topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The bouquet was offensively bright—a riot of crimson roses and shocking yellow lilies, all wrapped in crinkled, expensive cellophane. Elara stared at them on the kitchen counter, the silence of the apartment amplifying the frantic thumping in her chest. There was no note, no whisper of who had sent them, just the vibrant, unsolicited offering. Her hands, usually steady when working on her intricate pottery, trembled slightly as she traced the velvety texture of a deep red bloom. She quickly shoved the arrangement toward the back of a cabinet, desperate to hide the evidence of this bewildering gesture. Every instinct screamed at her to dispose of them, to make them vanish, because they felt like a spotlight illuminating every clumsy, unacknowledged part of her life.

[story 2]

When Liam walked in, the scent of lilies hit him first, sharp and overwhelming. He stopped dead in the doorway, his usual easy smile faltering. "Who sent these?" he asked, his voice tight. Sarah felt the blood drain from her face, leaving a cold, clammy feeling in her limbs. She mumbled something incoherent, picking at a loose thread on her sweater, avoiding his gaze entirely. The flowers sat on the entryway table, a silent accusation. She could feel the weight of expectation pressing down on her, a heavy cloak she couldn't seem to shrug off. She focused intensely on the pattern of the hardwood floor, willing herself to become invisible.

[story 3]

Marcus picked up the arrangement with two fingers, his touch surprisingly gentle, yet his eyes held a guarded flicker of something unreadable. He stood by the window, the afternoon sun catching the dew on the petals. He didn't speak immediately, just let the silence stretch, thick and uncomfortable. Marcus felt a profound internal tightening, a sudden awareness of every flaw he usually managed to mask with bravado. He quickly tucked the stems behind his back, his jaw clenching so hard his teeth ached. He needed to leave, needed distance from this unsolicited display, needing to retreat before the scrutiny of the vibrant, silent offering could truly settle upon him.
